items.title: Spoon Bring Out Hamilton Leithauser, Britt Daniel Joins Bodega In Central Park
items.summary: Three concert experiences I recommend: (1) attending a gig at Central Park SummerStage and going to see (2) Spoon or (3) Ratboys any chance you get. In June, I enjoyed all of the above. First, Ratboys kicked off their latest tour in my hometown of Columbus, showing off one of the most impressive indie rock catalogs in recent memory.
items.audio: audio/Spoon Bring Out Hamilton Leithauser_ Britt Daniel .mp3

items.title: Hannah Cole Announces Debut Album Switchbacks: Hear “MMA”
items.summary: Today, Nashville-based musician Hannah Cole announces her debut album Switchbacks with the down-on-her-luck single "MMA." Switchbacks, which is out in September via Tight Knit, follows 2024's Glisten EP and 2023's Big Bite EP. Cole reconnected with frequent collaborator Josef Kuhn, recording the album in their home studio between 2024 and 2025, playing nearly every instrument themselves.
items.audio: audio/Hannah Cole Announces Debut Album Switchbacks_ Hea.mp3

items.title: Cardinals Cover Iceage For SiriusXM
items.summary: Two of this year's best straight-up rock albums are Masquerade, the stormy debut from Irish Band To Watch Cardinals, and For Love Of Grace & The Hereafter, the excellently slithery new one from Danish vampires Iceage. During a recent live-in-studio session for SiriusXM, Cardinals paid tribute to their post-punk buzz-band elders by covering Iceage. The post Cardinals Cover Iceage For SiriusXM appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Between Cultures: How Ju Hyeon Kim Draws on Two Musical Traditions Through the Cello
items.summary: Classical music is often described as a universal language. The scores may remain the same whether they are performed in Seoul, New York, Berlin, or London, but the musicians bringing those compositions to life rarely share the same experiences. Every performer carries a unique history into the concert hall, and increasingly, those diverse perspectives are […]
